speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Gone to be married? Gone to swear a peace?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: False blood to false blood joined? Gone to be friends?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Shall Louis have Blanche and Blanche those provinces?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: It is not so. Thou hast misspoke, misheard.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Be well advised; tell o’er thy tale again.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: It cannot be; thou dost but say ’tis so.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: I trust I may not trust thee, for thy word

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Is but the vain breath of a common man.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Believe me, I do not believe thee, man.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: I have a king’s oath to the contrary.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Thou shalt be punished for thus flighting me,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: For I am sick and capable of fears,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Oppressed with wrongs and therefore full of fears,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: A widow, husbandless, subject to fears,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: A woman naturally born to fears.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And though thou now confess thou didst but jest,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: With my vexed spirits I cannot take a truce,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: But they will quake and tremble all this day.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: What dost thou mean by shaking of thy head?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Why dost thou look so sadly on my son?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: What means that hand upon that breast of thine?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Why holds thine eye that lamentable rheum,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Like a proud river peering o’er his bounds?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Be these sad signs confirmers of thy words?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Then speak again—not all thy former tale,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: But this one word, whether thy tale be true.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: As true as I believe you think them false

speaker: SALISBURY
text: That give you cause to prove my saying true.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: O, if thou teach me to believe this sorrow,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Teach thou this sorrow how to make me die,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And let belief and life encounter so

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: As doth the fury of two desperate men

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Which in the very meeting fall and die.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Louis marry Blanche?—O, boy, then where art thou?—

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: France friend with England? What becomes of me?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Fellow, be gone. I cannot brook thy sight.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: This news hath made thee a most ugly man.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: What other harm have I, good lady, done

speaker: SALISBURY
text: But spoke the harm that is by others done?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Which harm within itself so heinous is

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: As it makes harmful all that speak of it.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: I do beseech you, madam, be content.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: If thou that bidd’st me be content wert grim,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Ugly, and sland’rous to thy mother’s womb,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Full of unpleasing blots and sightless stains,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Lame, foolish, crooked, swart, prodigious,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Patched with foul moles and eye-offending marks,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: I would not care; I then would be content,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: For then I should not love thee; no, nor thou

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Become thy great birth, nor deserve a crown.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: But thou art fair, and at thy birth, dear boy,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Nature and Fortune joined to make thee great.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Of Nature’s gifts thou mayst with lilies boast,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And with the half-blown rose. But Fortune, O,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: She is corrupted, changed, and won from thee;

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Sh’ adulterates hourly with thine Uncle John,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And with her golden hand hath plucked on France

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: To tread down fair respect of sovereignty,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And made his majesty the bawd to theirs.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: France is a bawd to Fortune and King John,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: That strumpet Fortune, that usurping John.—

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Tell me, thou fellow, is not France forsworn?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Envenom him with words, or get thee gone

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And leave those woes alone which I alone

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Am bound to underbear.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Pardon me, madam,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: I may not go without you to the Kings.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Thou mayst, thou shalt, I will not go with thee.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: I will instruct my sorrows to be proud,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: For grief is proud and makes his owner stoop.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: To me and to the state of my great grief

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Let kings assemble, for my grief ’s ’s so great

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: That no supporter but the huge firm Earth

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Can hold it up. Here I and sorrows sit.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Here is my throne; bid kings come bow to it.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: ’Tis true, fair daughter, and this blessèd day

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Ever in France shall be kept festival.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: To solemnize this day the glorious sun

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Stays in his course and plays the alchemist,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Turning with splendor of his precious eye

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: The meager cloddy earth to glittering gold.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: The yearly course that brings this day about

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Shall never see it but a holy day.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: A wicked day, and not a holy day!

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: What hath this day deserved? What hath it done

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: That it in golden letters should be set

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Among the high tides in the calendar?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Nay, rather turn this day out of the week,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: This day of shame, oppression, perjury.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Or if it must stand still, let wives with child

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Pray that their burdens may not fall this day,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Lest that their hopes prodigiously be crossed.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: But on this day let seamen fear no wrack;

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: No bargains break that are not this day made;

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: This day, all things begun come to ill end,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Yea, faith itself to hollow falsehood change!

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: By heaven, lady, you shall have no cause

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: To curse the fair proceedings of this day.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Have I not pawned to you my majesty?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: You have beguiled me with a counterfeit

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Resembling majesty, which, being touched and tried,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Proves valueless. You are forsworn, forsworn.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: You came in arms to spill mine enemies’ blood,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: But now in arms you strengthen it with yours.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: The grappling vigor and rough frown of war

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Is cold in amity and painted peace,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And our oppression hath made up this league.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Arm, arm, you heavens, against these perjured kings!

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: A widow cries; be husband to me, God!

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Let not the hours of this ungodly day

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Wear out the days in peace, but ere sunset

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Set armèd discord ’twixt these perjured kings.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Hear me, O, hear me!

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Lady Constance, peace.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: War, war, no peace! Peace is to me a war.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: O Limoges, O Austria, thou dost shame

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: That bloody spoil. Thou slave, thou wretch, thou coward,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Thou little valiant, great in villainy,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Thou ever strong upon the stronger side,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Thou Fortune’s champion, that dost never fight

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: But when her humorous Ladyship is by

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: To teach thee safety. Thou art perjured too,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And sooth’st up greatness. What a fool art thou,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: A ramping fool, to brag and stamp and swear

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Upon my party. Thou cold-blooded slave,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Hast thou not spoke like thunder on my side?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Been sworn my soldier, bidding me depend

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Upon thy stars, thy fortune, and thy strength?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And dost thou now fall over to my foes?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Thou wear a lion’s hide! Doff it for shame,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And hang a calfskin on those recreant limbs.

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: O, that a man should speak those words to me!

speaker: BASTARD
text: “And hang a calfskin on those recreant limbs.”

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Thou dar’st not say so, villain, for thy life!

speaker: BASTARD
text: “And hang a calfskin on those recreant limbs.”

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: We like not this. Thou dost forget thyself.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Here comes the holy legate of the Pope.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Hail, you anointed deputies of heaven!

speaker: PANDULPH
text: To thee, King John, my holy errand is.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: I, Pandulph, of fair Milan cardinal

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And from Pope Innocent the legate here,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Do in his name religiously demand

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Why thou against the Church, our holy mother,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: So willfully dost spurn, and force perforce

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Keep Stephen Langton, chosen Archbishop

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Of Canterbury, from that Holy See.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: This, in our foresaid Holy Father’s name,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Pope Innocent, I do demand of thee.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: What earthy name to interrogatories

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Can task the free breath of a sacred king?

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Thou canst not, cardinal, devise a name

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: So slight, unworthy, and ridiculous

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: To charge me to an answer, as the Pope.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Tell him this tale, and from the mouth of England

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Add thus much more, that no Italian priest

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Shall tithe or toll in our dominions;

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: But as we under God are supreme head,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: So, under Him, that great supremacy

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Where we do reign we will alone uphold

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Without th’ assistance of a mortal hand.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: So tell the Pope, all reverence set apart

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: To him and his usurped authority.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Brother of England, you blaspheme in this.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Though you and all the kings of Christendom

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Are led so grossly by this meddling priest,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Dreading the curse that money may buy out,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: And by the merit of vile gold, dross, dust,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Purchase corrupted pardon of a man

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Who in that sale sells pardon from himself,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Though you and all the rest, so grossly led,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: This juggling witchcraft with revenue cherish,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Yet I alone, alone do me oppose

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Against the Pope, and count his friends my foes.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Then, by the lawful power that I have,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Thou shalt stand cursed and excommunicate;

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And blessèd shall he be that doth revolt

speaker: PANDULPH
text: From his allegiance to an heretic;

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And meritorious shall that hand be called,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Canonizèd and worshiped as a saint,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: That takes away by any secret course

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Thy hateful life.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: O, lawful let it be

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: That I have room with Rome to curse awhile!

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Good father cardinal, cry thou “Amen”

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: To my keen curses, for without my wrong

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: There is no tongue hath power to curse him right.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: There’s law and warrant, lady, for my curse.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And for mine, too. When law can do no right,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Let it be lawful that law bar no wrong.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Law cannot give my child his kingdom here,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: For he that holds his kingdom holds the law.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Therefore, since law itself is perfect wrong,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: How can the law forbid my tongue to curse?

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Philip of France, on peril of a curse,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Let go the hand of that arch-heretic,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And raise the power of France upon his head

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Unless he do submit himself to Rome.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: Look’st thou pale, France? Do not let go thy hand.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Look to that, devil, lest that France repent

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And by disjoining hands, hell lose a soul.

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: King Philip, listen to the Cardinal.

speaker: BASTARD
text: And hang a calfskin on his recreant limbs.

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Well, ruffian, I must pocket up these wrongs,

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Because—

speaker: BASTARD
text: Your breeches best may carry them.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Philip, what sayst thou to the Cardinal?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: What should he say, but as the Cardinal?

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Bethink you, father, for the difference

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Is purchase of a heavy curse from Rome,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Or the light loss of England for a friend.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Forgo the easier.

speaker: BLANCHE
text: That’s the curse of Rome.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: O Louis, stand fast! The devil tempts thee here

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: In likeness of a new untrimmèd bride.

speaker: BLANCHE
text: The Lady Constance speaks not from her faith,

speaker: BLANCHE
text: But from her need.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: O, if thou grant my need,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Which only lives but by the death of faith,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: That need must needs infer this principle:

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: That faith would live again by death of need.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: O, then tread down my need, and faith mounts up;

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Keep my need up, and faith is trodden down.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: The King is moved, and answers not to this.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: O, be removed from him, and answer well!

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Do so, King Philip. Hang no more in doubt.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Hang nothing but a calfskin, most sweet lout.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: I am perplexed and know not what to say.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: What canst thou say but will perplex thee more,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: If thou stand excommunicate and cursed?

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Good reverend father, make my person yours,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: And tell me how you would bestow yourself.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: This royal hand and mine are newly knit,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: And the conjunction of our inward souls

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Married, in league, coupled, and linked together

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: With all religious strength of sacred vows.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: The latest breath that gave the sound of words

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Was deep-sworn faith, peace, amity, true love

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Between our kingdoms and our royal selves;

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: And even before this truce, but new before,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: No longer than we well could wash our hands

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: To clap this royal bargain up of peace,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: God knows they were besmeared and overstained

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: With slaughter’s pencil, where revenge did paint

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: The fearful difference of incensèd kings.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: And shall these hands, so lately purged of blood,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: So newly joined in love, so strong in both,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Unyoke this seizure and this kind regreet?

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Play fast and loose with faith? So jest with heaven?

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Make such unconstant children of ourselves

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: As now again to snatch our palm from palm,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Unswear faith sworn, and on the marriage bed

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Of smiling peace to march a bloody host

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: And make a riot on the gentle brow

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Of true sincerity? O holy sir,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: My reverend father, let it not be so!

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Out of your grace, devise, ordain, impose

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Some gentle order, and then we shall be blest

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: To do your pleasure and continue friends.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: All form is formless, order orderless,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Save what is opposite to England’s love.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Therefore to arms! Be champion of our Church,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Or let the Church, our mother, breathe her curse,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: A mother’s curse, on her revolting son.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: France, thou mayst hold a serpent by the tongue,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: A chafèd lion by the mortal paw,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: A fasting tiger safer by the tooth,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Than keep in peace that hand which thou dost hold.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: I may disjoin my hand, but not my faith.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: So mak’st thou faith an enemy to faith,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And like a civil war sett’st oath to oath,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Thy tongue against thy tongue. O, let thy vow

speaker: PANDULPH
text: First made to God, first be to God performed,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: That is, to be the champion of our Church!

speaker: PANDULPH
text: What since thou swor’st is sworn against thyself

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And may not be performèd by thyself,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: For that which thou hast sworn to do amiss

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Is not amiss when it is truly done;

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And being not done where doing tends to ill,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: The truth is then most done not doing it.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: The better act of purposes mistook

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Is to mistake again; though indirect,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Yet indirection thereby grows direct,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And falsehood falsehood cures, as fire cools fire

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Within the scorchèd veins of one new-burned.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: It is religion that doth make vows kept,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: But thou hast sworn against religion

speaker: PANDULPH
text: By what thou swear’st against the thing thou swear’st,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And mak’st an oath the surety for thy truth

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Against an oath. The truth thou art unsure

speaker: PANDULPH
text: To swear swears only not to be forsworn,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Else what a mockery should it be to swear?

speaker: PANDULPH
text: But thou dost swear only to be forsworn,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And most forsworn to keep what thou dost swear.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Therefore thy later vows against thy first

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Is in thyself rebellion to thyself.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And better conquest never canst thou make

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Than arm thy constant and thy nobler parts

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Against these giddy loose suggestions,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Upon which better part our prayers come in,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: If thou vouchsafe them. But if not, then know

speaker: PANDULPH
text: The peril of our curses light on thee

speaker: PANDULPH
text: So heavy as thou shalt not shake them off,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: But in despair die under their black weight.

speaker: AUSTRIA
text: Rebellion, flat rebellion!

speaker: BASTARD
text: Will ’t not be?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Will not a calfskin stop that mouth of thine?

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Father, to arms!

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Upon thy wedding day?

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Against the blood that thou hast marrièd?

speaker: BLANCHE
text: What, shall our feast be kept with slaughtered men?

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Shall braying trumpets and loud churlish drums,

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Clamors of hell, be measures to our pomp?

speaker: BLANCHE
text: O husband, hear me! Ay, alack, how new

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Is “husband” in my mouth! Even for that name,

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Which till this time my tongue did ne’er pronounce,

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Upon my knee I beg, go not to arms

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Against mine uncle.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: O, upon my knee

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Made hard with kneeling, I do pray to thee,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Thou virtuous Dauphin, alter not the doom

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Forethought by heaven!

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Now shall I see thy love. What motive may

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Be stronger with thee than the name of wife?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: That which upholdeth him that thee upholds,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: His honor.—O, thine honor, Louis, thine honor!

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: I muse your Majesty doth seem so cold,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: When such profound respects do pull you on.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: I will denounce a curse upon his head.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Thou shalt not need.—England, I will fall from thee.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: O, fair return of banished majesty!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              ELEANOR
text: O, foul revolt of French inconstancy!

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: France, thou shalt rue this hour within this hour.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Old Time the clock-setter, that bald sexton Time,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Is it as he will? Well, then, France shall rue.

speaker: BLANCHE
text: The sun’s o’ercast with blood. Fair day, adieu.

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Which is the side that I must go withal?

speaker: BLANCHE
text: I am with both, each army hath a hand,

speaker: BLANCHE
text: And in their rage, I having hold of both,

speaker: BLANCHE
text: They whirl asunder and dismember me.

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Husband, I cannot pray that thou mayst win.—

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Uncle, I needs must pray that thou mayst lose.—

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Father, I may not wish the fortune thine.—

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Grandam, I will not wish thy wishes thrive.

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Whoever wins, on that side shall I lose.

speaker: BLANCHE
text: Assurèd loss before the match be played.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Lady, with me, with me thy fortune lies.

speaker: BLANCHE
text: There where my fortune lives, there my life dies.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: Cousin, go draw our puissance together.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: France, I am burned up with inflaming wrath,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: A rage whose heat hath this condition,

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: That nothing can allay, nothing but blood—

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: The blood, and dearest-valued blood, of France.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Thy rage shall burn thee up, and thou shalt turn

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: To ashes ere our blood shall quench that fire.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Look to thyself. Thou art in jeopardy.

speaker:
KING
               
              JOHN
text: No more than he that threats.—To arms let’s hie!
